title: Restore Lost Low-Bits from Binary Array

statement.markdown:
Given a binary array `nums` where every `nums[i]` is in the range `[0, 1]`, restore lost low-bits to make all numbers in `nums` as large as possible. You can add any `1` in the array to make the number larger. For example, if `nums = [0, 1, 0, 1]`, adding `1` at the end will make the number larger than adding `1` in the first place, so we should add `1` at the end.

Return the minimum number of `1`s to add to make all numbers in `nums` as large as possible.
statement.inputFormat: `nums`: a binary array of size `n` where every `nums[i]` is in the range `[0, 1]`
statement.outputFormat: The minimum number of `1`s to add to make all numbers in `nums` as large as possible
statement.notes: You can add any `1` in the array to make the number larger.

examples.explanation: Adding 1 at the end will make the number larger than adding 1 in the first place, so we should add 1 at the end.

hints.text: Start from the right side of the array and try to find the maximum bits that can be added without changing the number of 1s added
